# BambooHR API

**OpenAPI spec included** · [View spec →](./v1/openapi.json) · *Generated by Jentic — the vendor does not publish an official OpenAPI specification.*

Manage employee records, time-off requests, benefits enrollment, payroll data, and time tracking for small-to-medium businesses through 126 endpoints.

| | |
|---|---|
| **Base URL** | `https://api.bamboohr.com/api/gateway.php/{subdomain}` |
| **Auth** | Basic Auth |
| **Endpoints** | 126 |

## What you can build with this API

- Pull a full employee directory and retrieve individual employee records with custom field data
- Submit and approve time-off requests with balance calculations across leave types
- Enroll employees in benefit plans and manage group plan assignments and costs
- Export payroll data including compensation tables, deductions, and pay stubs
- Track employee clock-in/clock-out entries and calculate hours worked per pay period

Does not handle recruiting, applicant tracking, or onboarding workflows — use it for core HR data, leave, benefits, payroll, and time tracking.

## Key endpoints

| Method | Path | Description |
|--------|------|-------------|
| GET | /v1/employees/directory | Get employee directory |
| GET | /v1/employees/{id} | Get employee details |
| POST | /v1/employees/ | Add an employee |
| GET | /v1/employees/{id}/tables/compensation | Get compensation table for an employee |
| POST | /v1/time_off/requests | Submit a time-off request |
| GET | /v1/payroll | Get payroll data |

## Key resources

| Resource | Description |
|----------|-------------|
| Employees | Add, retrieve, and update employee records and directory listings |
| Time Off | Submit, approve, and query time-off requests and balances |
| Benefits | Manage benefit groups, plans, costs, and employee enrollment |
| Payroll | Export compensation data, deductions, and pay stubs |
| Time Tracking | Record and query clock entries and hours worked |
| Tables | Access structured employee data like compensation and job history |

### Python quickstart

Your agent can search for this API by what it does, inspect the schema, and execute operations — without ever handling API keys. Jentic stores credentials in a vault and injects them at runtime.

```python
import asyncio
from jentic import Jentic, SearchRequest, LoadRequest, ExecutionRequest

async def main():
    client = Jentic()
    search = await client.search(SearchRequest(query="get employee directory and time-off balances"))
    entity_id = search.results[0].id
    details = await client.load(LoadRequest(ids=[entity_id]))
    result = await client.execute(
        ExecutionRequest(
            id=entity_id,
            inputs={"subdomain": "mycompany"}
        )
    )
    print(result)

asyncio.run(main())
```

Works with LangChain, CrewAI, OpenAI Agents SDK, Claude Agent SDK, and any MCP-compatible framework.
